methods.description: Process for collecting ice core samples from Mount Johns and transporting them to the Climate Change Institute for further processing.

steps.description: The Mount Johns core was recovered by a Brazilian team from Centro Polar e Climático in the 2008/2009 austral summer.
steps.searchable_summary: recover ice core from Mount Johns

steps.description: The core was 92 m of firn and ice in 94 sections of approximately 1 m length and 8.5 cm diameter.
All sections were packed in polyethylene bags and placed in Styrofoam boxes for transport at temperatures below -15ºC.
steps.searchable_summary: pack and transport ice core sections

methods.description: Methodology for processing ice core samples using continuous melting with discrete sampling (CMDS) and analyzing them for various isotopic and elemental compositions.

steps.description: The ice core processing consists of preparation, followed by melting and collection of discrete sections, as analysable aliquots.
steps.searchable_summary: prepare and melt ice core for analysis

steps.description: Cutting, thinning, decontaminating and holding the sections in polymethyl methacrylate melter supports followed a stratigraphic and cleaning protocol control (ISO 14644-1) under class 5 laminar airflow hoods in an environment below -20ºC.
steps.searchable_summary: cut, thin, and decontaminate ice core sections

steps.description: Melting and sub-sampling occurred entirely within a class 7 clean room equipped with class 5 laminar airflow hoods where sit the collectors (auto samplers) used to separate the co-registered samples for ICP-MS and IC analysis.
steps.searchable_summary: melt and sub-sample ice core in clean room

steps.description: The external portion of the ice core was co-sampled in the same way for determination of the stable isotope ratios (δD and δ18O) by the cavity ring-down spectroscopy (CRDS) technique.
steps.searchable_summary: analyze stable isotope ratios using CRDS

steps.description: The core was fully melted and sub-sampled with a mean stratigraphic resolution of 2.1 cm.
steps.searchable_summary: melt and sub-sample ice core with 2.1 cm resolution

steps.description: The samples and blanks in polypropylene vials were stored below -15ºC in a cold room, packed for thermic transport and sent ready to be analysed at the Centro Polar e Climático laboratory in Porto Alegre (Brazil), by an ion exchange chromatography (IEC) system described below.
steps.searchable_summary: store and transport samples for IEC analysis
